campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss staffing vacancies and recruitment efforts for city positions, held a public hearing on a government matter, and made announcements about the upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matter
Council reports and public comment period

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — June 3, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council met to decide which sheriff service options to use for the upcoming fiscal year 2026. Once the council picks their preferred approach, they plan to give staff direction on next steps.
keyTopics: Choosing between different sheriff service level options for FY 2026
Council's near-term strategy for sheriff services
Staff direction to be provided after service level selection

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve financial reports, authorize the town manager to sign several service agreements and construction contracts, and adopt a list of projects for the upcoming fiscal year. Key decisions included approving a paving contract with O'Grady Paving and applying for state grants to fund community projects.
keyTopics: Approved March 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized O'Grady Paving construction contract
Approved five-year service agreements for town services and HouseKeys
Adopted list of projects for Fiscal Year 2026-27 with state funding

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 16, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve routine administrative matters including committee records and staff appointments, settled a legal claim involving Gordon Biersch Brewing Company, and discussed finances for the hotel business district and a downtown property acquisition on South First Street.
keyTopics: Settlement agreement with Gordon Biersch Brewing Company
Hotel Business Improvement District assessment levy for 2026-2027
Real property acquisition at 26-36 South First Street
New board and commission member appointments

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a closed-door meeting on June 23, 2026, to discuss hiring a new City Attorney and to consult with lawyers about ongoing and potential lawsuits involving the city. These items were discussed privately rather than in public because they involve personnel decisions and sensitive legal matters.
keyTopics: City Attorney hiring/appointment
Baykeeper lawsuit against Mountain View and Sunnyvale
Anticipated litigation with significant legal risk to the city

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The closed session typically involves confidential personnel, legal, or real estate matters that aren't discussed publicly.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting
Regular council meeting convened after closed session
Council salute to the flag

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 15, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting with legal counsel to discuss an anticipated legal case the city may file. The council also discussed lobbying registration requirements that apply to people who try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: Potential litigation the city is considering initiating
Lobbying registration and reporting rules under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
Requirements for people who attempt to influence city legislative or administrative actions to register

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 23, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a hybrid meeting on June 23, 2026, allowing residents to attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The council provided multiple ways for the public to submit comments and participate in the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and Zoom)
Remote participation via Zoom at meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Written public comments accepted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve routine administrative matters and consider updates to the city's federal housing and community development funding plan. The council also reviewed requests from individual councilmembers for new work items and discussed a change to how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Updated federal housing funding plan for 2025-2026
Changes to the council vacancy process
New work requests from individual councilmembers
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing on a plan to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be permanently affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is requesting special allowances under state law to make the project work on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units in a 6-story building on 0.43 acres
Project replaces 52-space city parking lot (Lot T)
Developer requesting density bonus concessions under state law
Environmental review underway for potential exemption
